summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a stellar May ’77 performance, highlighted by a smoking Half‑Step Mississippi, flawless Jack Straw and an unforgettable Scarlet > Fire/Other One → Wharf Rat sequence.
review: The Charlie Miller SBD remaster delivers crystal‑clear audio that lets the band’s 1977 magic shine through.  Fans rave about the tight first set – Half‑Step Mississippi, Jack Straw, Peggy‑O and a superb Scarlet > Fire – and a second‑set marathon of The Other One/Wharf Rat, Estimated and Lazy Lightning→Supplication that many rank among the month’s best.  Jerry’s vocals and lead work are repeatedly praised, while Phil’s bass, Keith’s piano/organ touches and the dual drums lock in perfectly.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly positive (mostly 5‑star) praising both musical performance and audio fidelity; only a handful of neutral/low ratings, giving an overall rating in the excellent range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with very little hiss or muddiness; reviewers repeatedly call it ‘crisp’, ‘near‑perfect’, ‘awesome SBD’ and note the Miller remaster sounds better than other feeds.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight, work‑manlike first set that builds into a second‑set marathon of jam highlights; the transition from The Other One into Wharf Rat and Wheel is repeatedly called a ‘sandwich’ that lifts the show to classic status.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Virtually every review praises his guitar tone, soaring solos and spot‑on vocals – described as perfect, soulful and heart‑warming.
band_member_comments.Phil: Frequently noted for strong, punchy bass lines that ‘fat­ten up the sound’ and for shining throughout both sets.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm work and vocal contributions are highlighted as solid, especially on Jack Straw; reviewers say he sounded happy and in sync with the band.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s piano is lauded for emotional depth on ballads (He’s Gone, Peggy‑O); a few fans mention Brett Weir’s organ work as a pleasant contrast.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart are praised for being ‘locked in’, delivering tight grooves that drive the Scarlet>Fire and jam sections.
